title: Outpatient Slot Allocation Multiclinic
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: outpatient slot allocation multiclinic.
assumptions: Each clinic_slot and named resource is single-booking and must be reserved and released explicitly.
Administrative documents and consents (consent_document) are authoritative prerequisites for progressing certain actions.
Service profiles fully capture resource bundles required (clinician, room, equipment, diagnostic slots).
Inter-clinic travel times and patient transit logistics are out of scope for slot allocation (logistics simplified).
Providers and staff availability indicated in the initial facts are accurate and timely updated by reservation actions.
risks: Resource contention across clinics if concurrent reservations are attempted without centralized arbitration.
Incomplete or delayed administrative documentation leading to blocked confirmations or last-minute cancellations.
Mismatch between service_profile requirements and actual allocated resources (e.g., missing equipment).
No-shows or late cancellations causing cascading slot underutilization not handled by this workflow.
Inconsistent updates to telehealth endpoints or diagnostic slots causing double-booking or unavailable appointments.
